original_query: select o_year, sum(case when nation = 'CANADA' then volume else 0 end) / sum(volume) as mkt_share from ( select extract(year from o_orderdate) as o_year, l_extendedprice * (1 - l_discount) as volume, n2.n_name as nation from part, supplier, lineitem, orders, customer, nation n1, nation n2, region where p_partkey = l_partkey and s_suppkey = l_suppkey and l_orderkey = o_orderkey and o_custkey = c_custkey and c_nationkey = n1.n_nationkey and n1.n_regionkey = r_regionkey and r_name = 'AMERICA' and s_nationkey = n2.n_nationkey and o_orderdate between date '1995-01-01' and date '1996-12-31' and p_type = 'ECONOMY PLATED STEEL' ) as all_nations group by o_year order by o_year;
rewritten_query:
SELECT 
    EXTRACT(year FROM o_orderdate) AS o_year,
    SUM(CASE WHEN n2.n_name = 'CANADA' THEN l_extendedprice * (1 - l_discount) ELSE 0 END) / SUM(l_extendedprice * (1 - l_discount)) AS mkt_share
FROM region
JOIN nation n1 ON n1.n_regionkey = r_regionkey AND r_name = 'AMERICA'
JOIN customer ON c_nationkey = n1.n_nationkey
JOIN orders ON o_custkey = c_custkey AND o_orderdate BETWEEN DATE '1995-01-01' AND DATE '1996-12-31'
JOIN lineitem ON l_orderkey = o_orderkey
JOIN part ON p_partkey = l_partkey AND p_type = 'ECONOMY PLATED STEEL'
JOIN supplier ON s_suppkey = l_suppkey
JOIN nation n2 ON s_nationkey = n2.n_nationkey
GROUP BY o_year
ORDER BY o_year;

rewrite_suggestion.produced_suggestion: Replaced implicit joins with explicit JOIN syntax to improve readability and help the optimizer understand table relationships better
Restructured the query to eliminate the subquery, allowing the optimizer to potentially choose a better join order
Moved the CASE expression directly into the SUM aggregation to avoid redundant calculations
